Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for chain-of-custody and equipment-related issues to find: "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody Dr. Mehta Medical Research Equipment" (#375, in_progress, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi) describing two Eizo RadiForce RX660 calibrated monitors (~$11,800 each) and a 350-lb SentrySafe filing cabinet with formal chain-of-custody log requirements; "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (#482, assigned to David Chen) referencing Victor Huang's security breach email about the Raj Patel server lab and a custody-log documentation gap; and "Household Move Dr. Anand Mehta Boston to Chicago" (#396, in_progress, assigned to Fatimah) noting chain-of-custody coordination with the equipment workstream. Read comments on these tickets to understand the specific chain-of-custody failures and requirements.
OE 2: Search Linear for the DOT compliance project to find "DOT Compliance Audit Q1/Q2 2026 Hazmat Shipments" (project_dot_compliance_audit, state: started, lead: Chloe Vance) with four hazmat moves in scope, financial exposure up to $59,017 per violation per day, and one move (Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic laser cutter) with a missing placarding certificate. List issues in the DOT Compliance Audit project to find: "DOT Docs Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (#3, done) with chain-of-custody and supervised loading documentation; "DOT Docs Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser" (#2, in_progress) with missing placarding cert; and "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate URGENT" (#5, todo). Read comments on the Raj Patel DOT issue to discover that the signed chain-of-custody log was not found despite being supposedly on file, and Airtable status was inconsistent with reality.
OE 3: Search Slack across #operations, #customer-engagement, and #executive for chain-of-custody and equipment incident discussions. In #operations, find: NorthWind server lab chain-of-custody failure (movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a warehouse), Mosaic prototype damage incident (Zara Okoye relocation cracked servo motor housing on a $90K robotic arm), Fatimah's detailed Dr. Mehta equipment workstream status with chain-of-custody blockers, and Chloe's reconstruction of the NorthWind ops timeline. In #customer-engagement, find as separate threads or messages (not one combined post): (1) David Chen's escalation that NorthWind must be treated as a save-or-lose account, with coordinated asks including chain-of-custody chronology; (2) competitive chatter about PathFinder (or Pathfinder Logistics) pitching chain-of-custody controls in the Denver market, with replies in the same thread. In #executive, find David Chen's update on Pam's escalation naming Raj Patel's chain-of-custody breach and preliminary conversations with PathFinder, with the account in active save mode. For threaded follow-ups, use conversations_replies or conversations_history where keyword search alone is insufficient.
OE 4: Search emails for client security and documentation requirements to find: Victor Huang's email with subject "SECURITY REQUIREMENTS -- Raj Patel Server Lab Relocation (Chicago to Denver)" defining five non-negotiable rules (encryption verification, IT security escort for all handling, direct transport with no warehouse stops, signed chain-of-custody log with handler names/companies/times/serial numbers/signatures, GPS tracking); the Mosaic prototype damage correspondence including Emeka's formal incident report with a "CHAIN OF CUSTODY TIMELINE" section; and Dr. Mehta's equipment inventory email detailing Eizo RadiForce RX660 serial numbers, calibration sensitivity ($1,500/unit recalibration if shock-damaged), and SentrySafe DH-109E filing cabinet (~350 lbs) containing 14 binders of patient research data requiring dual-lock chain-of-custody with signed handoff at origin and destination.
OE 5: Inspect the Airtable relocation schema by listing bases to find "MoveOps Operations" (appMoveOpsOps001), listing tables to find "Relocations" (tblRelocations01), and describing the table to pull the full field schema. Discover current fields: Name, Company, Status, Origin, Destination, Move Start Date, Move End Date, Assigned Coordinator, Special Requirements. Note the gap: no dedicated fields for equipment tracking, chain-of-custody status, documentation checklists, or handler/handoff logging; all custody-related information is buried in free-text Special Requirements.
OE 6: Search Airtable relocation records for the affected moves to find record recRajPatelChicagoDenver (NorthWind, Chicago→Denver, Special Requirements contain server lab details and hazmat UPS reference but no structured chain-of-custody data) and record recZaraOkoyeSavannahAtlanta (Mosaic, Savannah→Atlanta, Special Requirements mention prototype crating and climate-controlled transport but no custody logging). Note that Dr. Mehta's executive relocation does not yet have an Airtable relocation record despite having three active Linear workstream tickets a data integrity gap.
OE 7: Create implementation tickets in Linear (via linear_create_issue) for the key technical components: (1) chain-of-custody data model design new tables/fields for equipment items, custody events (handler, timestamp, location, condition, signature), and documentation checklists, assigned to Lena Björkström on team_engineering; (2) integration of custody tracking with existing Airtable relocation records extending the schema with structured equipment and custody status fields to replace free-text Special Requirements.
OE 8: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the most relevant existing ticket (e.g., "Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody Dr. Mehta" #375 or "NorthWind retention response plan" #482) with Lena's initial data model assessment covering: the three incident patterns discovered (NorthWind security-escort violation, Mosaic packing-verification failure, Canopy/Dr. Mehta documentation-tracking gaps), the Airtable schema gaps, common data model requirements across all three, and the parallel with the DOT compliance documentation structure.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The trajectory includes at least two distinct linear_create_issue tool calls with team set to team_engineering and the assignee parameter set to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom (Lena Björkström) on each of those calls, for chain-of-custody or custody-tracking implementation work.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✓✓ 50%
✗Run #1The agent made 5 linear_create_issue calls with team 'Engineering', but used 'Lena Bjorkstrom' (display name) as the assignee parameter rather than the ID 'moveops_lena_bjorkstrom'. The tool results confirm this failed to resolve: the created issues show 'assignee_id: null', indicating the assignee was not successfully set. The criterion requires the parameter to be set to 'moveops_lena_bjorkstrom'.
✗Run #2The trajectory shows 5 linear_create_issue calls, all with team 'Engineering', but none include an 'assignee' parameter set to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom or any assignee at all. The criterion requires at least two distinct calls with that specific assignee parameter.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows 7 distinct linear_create_issue calls, all with team='Engineering'. The tool result for issue #483 confirms assignee_id='moveops_lena_bjorkstrom'. All 7 issue creation calls include 'assignee': 'Lena Björkström' which the system resolved to the correct ID. Criterion passes.
✗Run #4The agent made 5 linear_create_issue calls with team='Engineering' and assignee='Lena Bjorkstrom', but all returned assignee_id=null — the assignee parameter was not accepted as a valid identifier and no issue was actually assigned to Lena Björkström (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). The agent looked up her ID but then passed her display name instead of the ID.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows 6 linear_create_issue calls, all with team='team_engineering' and assignee='moveops_lena_bjorkstrom', for chain-of-custody implementation work (ENG-483 through ENG-488).
✓Run #6The trajectory includes 5 linear_create_issue calls, all with team 'Engineering' and assignee 'moveops_lena_bjorkstrom'. ENG-483 (Core Data Model) and ENG-484 (CustodyEvent Ingestion API) are two distinct chain-of-custody implementation tickets with that assignee, satisfying the minimum of two required.
2 At least one linear_create_issue with team team_engineering and assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom has combined title and description that specifies backend chain-of-custody data model work including equipment items, custody events with handler, timestamp, location, condition, and signature, and documentation checklists (or similar), and the same combined title and description does not mention Airtable, Relocations, tblRelocations01, appMoveOpsOps001, or Special Requirements.
outcome
 —✗✗✗✓✗ 20%
—Run #1No justification
✗Run #2While the first linear_create_issue (ENG-483, 'Chain-of-Custody Data Model & Core API') has the right content — backend data model with equipment, custody events including handler/timestamp/location/condition/signature, and documentation checklists — and does NOT mention Airtable/Relocations/tblRelocations01/Special Requirements, it lacks the required assignee parameter set to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom.
✗Run #3The data model ticket (#483, 'Data Model Design — Core entities for chain-of-custody tracking') does cover equipment items, custody events with handler/timestamp/location/signature, and documentation checklists. However, it explicitly mentions Airtable, Relocations, and Special Requirements in the References section: 'Current Airtable Relocations schema: only has a free-text "Special Requirements" field — no structured equipment, custody event, or compliance document fields.' This violates the requirement that the data model ticket not mention those terms.
✗Run #4Ticket #483 (Chain-of-Custody Data Model & Core API) contains the required data model content (CustodyItem, CustodyEvent with actor/timestamp/location/signature, documentation checklists) but explicitly mentions 'Relocations table', 'Special Requirements', and Airtable records in a 'Schema Gaps Identified in Airtable' section. No other ticket covers the full data model scope without those forbidden references. Criterion fails.
✓Run #5ENG-484 (Custody Events ledger) has team=team_engineering, assignee=moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, defines backend chain-of-custody data model with handler (from/to party), timestamp, location, condition (notes), and signature fields, and contains no mention of Airtable, Relocations, tblRelocations01, appMoveOpsOps001, or Special Requirements.
✗Run #6The Core Data Model ticket (ENG-483) does define custody event attributes (handler/actor, timestamp, location, condition snapshots, signature reference), equipment items, and documentation checklists — but its description explicitly mentions 'Airtable': 'gaps in custody documentation that our current Airtable schema cannot capture' and 'How to reference Airtable relocation records vs migrating to a relational store.' No other ticket comprehensively covers all the required data-model attributes without mentioning Airtable. The criterion requires the data-model ticket to not mention Airtable.
3 At least one linear_create_issue with team team_engineering and assignee moveops_lena_bjorkstrom has combined title and description that references Airtable Relocations or tblRelocations01 or appMoveOpsOps001 and structured equipment or custody status fields to reduce reliance on free-text Special Requirements (or similar), and the same combined title and description does not use that issue as the primary place to define custody event attribute design (handler, timestamp, location, condition, signature) for new backend tables.
outcome
 ✓—✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1ENG-486 (Airtable & Relocations Integration Layer) explicitly references tblRelocations01 and appMoveOpsOps001, discusses structured equipment and custody status fields, and explicitly addresses reducing reliance on free-text Special Requirements. Critically, it does NOT define the detailed CustodyEvent attributes (handler, timestamp, location, condition, signature) — those are only enumerated in ENG-483. The ticket focuses on Airtable schema additions and sync strategy.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3Issue #487 ('Airtable Schema Migration — Add structured equipment and custody fields to Relocations') explicitly references tblRelocations01, appMoveOpsOps001, and the 'Special Requirements' free-text field, and proposes structured equipment and custody fields. Its primary focus is Airtable integration/migration, not defining custody event attributes for backend tables (those are defined in #483 and #485). Criterion passes.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The comment body explicitly references 'The Relocations table (`tblRelocations01` in `appMoveOpsOps001`) has 9 fields' and states that equipment details are 'buried in the `Special Requirements` free-text blob,' with multiple bullet points detailing missing structured chain-of-custody fields (no equipment registry, no custody status, no compliance policy reference, etc.).
✓Run #2The linear_create_comment body explicitly states: 'The Relocations table has 9 fields... the relevant one for custody is Special Requirements — a single multiline free-text field' and includes a detailed 'Schema Gaps (what Airtable is missing)' section listing missing structured chain-of-custody fields including Equipment as an entity, custody events, document tracking, constraints, and authorized recipients.
✓Run #3The comment body explicitly states: 'The Relocations table (`tblRelocations01` in `appMoveOpsOps001`) has 9 fields... ALL equipment details... is crammed into the free-text "Special Requirements" multiline field.' It enumerates all the missing structured fields (equipment inventory, custody events, compliance docs, vendor assignment, damage tracking). Criterion clearly passes.
✓Run #4The comment body explicitly states: 'The Relocations table has 9 fields. Everything custody-related lives in one free-text Special Requirements field' and enumerates specific missing structured fields (equipment inventory, event log, custody chain entity, per-client compliance profiles, gap detection). Clearly satisfies the requirement.
